item.title: Microsoft’s AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The push for new data centers at Microsoft is putting one of the its key clean power goals at risk.

item.title: Robinhood’s venture fund IPO attracted 150,000+ retail investors, CEO says
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev says more than 150,000 retail investors joined the fintech’s new venture fund, which offers exposure to private tech companies like OpenAI, Stripe, Databricks, and Oura before they go public.

item.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

item.title: DOJ says ransomware gang tapped into Russian government databases
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: U.S. prosecutors said a ransomware gang fueled Russian government corruption, and allowed the gang's leaders to avoid paying taxes and dodge the country's military draft.

item.title: AI evaluation startup Braintrust confirms breach, tells every customer to rotate sensitive keys
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Braintrust, a startup that makes an “operating system for engineers building AI software,” notified customers that hackers broke into one of its Amazon cloud environments, and is asking customers to rotate their API keys.

item.title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

item.title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like those from OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Chrome on Android now supports approximate instead of precise location sharing
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The new feature is a small win for Android users, as it gives them more control over how much location data they share with websites.

item.title: Google updates AI search to include quotes from Reddit and other sources
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: While citing web forums and discussion boards can help users find answers to more niche queries, this design choice could also prove chaotic.

item.title: Khosla-backed robotics startup Genesis AI has gone full stack, demo shows
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Genesis AI, a startup that raised a $105 million seed round to build foundational AI for robotics, has unveiled its first model, GENE-26.5, but also a demo showcasing a set of robotic hands performing complex tasks.

item.title: Tinder owner Match Group is slowing hiring to pay for its increased use of AI tools
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Match Group said that it's slowing its hiring plans for the rest of the year because AI tools "cost a lot of money."

item.title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit for overpromising the arrival of Siri's AI features.

item.title: Ethos raises $22.75M from a16z for its expert network with voice onboarding
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Ethos says it is onboarding 35,000 experts per week.

item.title: Aurora lands McLane deal to run driverless truck routes in Texas
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Aurora said it plans to expand to new routes between McLane distribution centers across the U.S. Sun Belt by the end of 2026.

item.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, all your M&A questions will be answered
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Leaders from Coinbase, M13, and Mignano Law Group talk about how M&A is an early-stage strategy at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Register to hear this live.

item.title: 3 days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Three days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to Disrupt 2026. Buy one TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket, and get a second ticket at 50% off. Gain more visibility in the tech industry. Offer ends May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: AI boom pushes Samsung to $1T
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Samsung crossed the $1 trillion valuation mark after shares surged on AI-driven chip demand, making it only the second Asian company after TSMC to hit the milestone.

item.title: Some kids are bypassing age-verification checks with a fake mustache
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: A new survey found that kids find it easy to bypass age checks, despite a rise in age-verification laws around the world.

item.title: reMarkable’s new Paper Pure tablet goes back to basics with a monochrome screen
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The new Paper Pure is lighter and faster than the reMarkable 2, which is going to be retired six years since its launch.

item.title: Peter Sarlin’s QuTwo reaches $380M valuation in angel round
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Enterprise AI will be QuTwo’s bread and butter. “AI is the north star that we will continue to aim for. Quantum is just a new type of compute,” said Sarlin, who is adamant that QuTwo is an AI company.
